courseTitle: Location Map Drawing — Municipal Submission Standards
courseDescription: Complete guide to creating professional location maps for municipal submissions. Covers site location, surrounding roads, landmarks, north arrow, scale, GPS coordinates, Google Maps tracing, and municipality-required formatting for building permits and land development proposals.

practicalExamples.title: Google Maps Screenshot Method
practicalExamples.description: Take screenshot from Google Maps and trace in AutoCAD
steps: Go to Google Maps and find your project location
Set map view to satellite or hybrid mode
Take screenshot at appropriate zoom level
Insert screenshot in AutoCAD using IMAGEATTACH command
Scale the image using known reference points
Trace roads, boundaries, and landmarks on separate layers
Remove or hide the background image when tracing is complete

practicalExamples.title: GPS Coordinate Method
practicalExamples.description: Use precise GPS coordinates for accurate positioning
steps: Obtain GPS coordinates for site corners using GPS device or smartphone
Use online tools like GPS Coordinate Converter for UTM conversion
In AutoCAD, use POINT command to mark coordinate positions
Connect points using PLINE to create accurate boundaries
Verify coordinates using GEOGRAPHICLOCATION command in AutoCAD
Add coordinate annotations for municipal reference

practicalExamples.title: Survey Data Integration
practicalExamples.description: Import professional survey data for highest accuracy
steps: Obtain survey data from licensed surveyor (DXF or DWG format)
Import survey data using INSERT or XREF command
Verify coordinate system and units match your drawing standards
Clean up survey data keeping only relevant information
Add your project elements based on accurate survey base
Include survey license number and date in title block

realWorldExamples.description: Commercial building location map in busy urban area
Industrial facility location in eastern Nepal

courseObjectives: Create accurate location maps showing project site within surrounding area
Include essential municipal requirements: north arrow, scale, legend, and title block
Draw surrounding roads, landmarks, and reference points clearly
Produce print-ready location maps meeting municipal submission standards

phases.phaseTitle: Setup — Drawing Environment and Reference Data
phases.phaseDescription: Prepare your drawing file with correct units, layers, and reference materials.

steps.title: Set Up Drawing File and Units
steps.subtitle: Configure for location map scale and units
steps.why: Location maps typically use larger scales (1:2000, 1:5000) and require different unit setup than building plans.
instructions: Type UNITS and set Type to Decimal, Insertion Scale to Meters for large-scale maps
Set Precision to 0.00 for meter-level accuracy
Use LIMITS to set appropriate drawing area (e.g., 0,0 to 1000,1000 for 1km x 1km area)
Save as Location_Map_Template.dwt for reuse

steps.title: Create Location Map Layers
steps.subtitle: Organize different map elements
steps.why: Proper layer organization makes it easy to control visibility and print different map versions.
instructions: Create layers: SITE_BOUNDARY (Red, 0.50mm), ROADS (Yellow, 0.40mm), BUILDINGS (White, 0.30mm)
Add layers: LANDMARKS (Cyan, 0.25mm), TEXT (White, 0.18mm), NORTH_ARROW (Green, 0.40mm)
Create layers: SCALE_BAR (Magenta, 0.35mm), GRID (Gray, 0.09mm), TITLE_BLOCK (Blue, 0.25mm)
Set appropriate colors and lineweights for professional appearance

phases.phaseTitle: Base Map — Site Boundary and Surrounding Area
phases.phaseDescription: Draw the project site boundary and surrounding infrastructure.

steps.title: Draw Project Site Boundary
steps.subtitle: Define the exact project limits using GPS or survey data
steps.why: The site boundary clearly shows the project area and must be accurate for legal and planning purposes.
instructions: Set current layer to SITE_BOUNDARY
Method 1: GPS Coordinates - Use POINT command to mark GPS coordinates, then connect with PLINE
Method 2: Google Maps Tracing - Insert screenshot, scale using reference points, then trace boundary
Method 3: Survey Data - Import professional survey coordinates using absolute X,Y format
Close the polyline to create a complete boundary
Add GPS coordinate annotations at key corners (e.g., '27.7172°N, 85.3240°E')
Calculate and annotate total site area using AREA command

practicalTip.title: GPS Coordinate Accuracy
practicalTip.text: For municipal submissions, use professional GPS devices with ±3m accuracy. Smartphone GPS is acceptable for preliminary drawings only. Always verify coordinates with multiple readings.

steps.title: Draw Surrounding Roads and Streets
steps.subtitle: Show access routes using Google Maps reference and field verification
steps.why: Road access is critical for municipal approval and shows how the site connects to the infrastructure network.
instructions: Set current layer to ROADS
Reference Method: Use Google Maps screenshot to trace road centerlines and edges
Field Verification: Measure actual road widths on-site (typically 6-12m for main roads)
Draw main roads first using PLINE with measured widths
Use OFFSET to create road edges and medians
Add road names, street numbers, and highway route numbers
Include pedestrian pathways and bicycle lanes if applicable
Show traffic direction indicators for one-way streets

steps.title: Add Surrounding Buildings and Structures
steps.subtitle: Show context and neighboring properties
steps.why: Context buildings help reviewers understand the project's relationship with existing development.
instructions: Set current layer to BUILDINGS
Draw neighboring building footprints using RECTANG or PLINE
Use simple rectangles for existing buildings - detailed architecture not needed
Add important landmarks like schools, hospitals, temples using LANDMARKS layer
Use HATCH with different patterns to distinguish between building types if required

phases.phaseTitle: Map Elements — North Arrow, Scale, and Grid
phases.phaseDescription: Add essential map elements required for municipal submission.

steps.title: Add Professional North Arrow
steps.subtitle: Standard north direction indicator
steps.why: North arrow is mandatory for all location maps and must point to true north, not magnetic north.
instructions: Set current layer to NORTH_ARROW
Insert a professional north arrow block (create one if not available)
Place north arrow in a corner where it doesn't obscure important details
Ensure arrow points to true north (top of map typically)
Add 'N' label if not included in the block
Size should be proportional to map scale - typically 20-30mm at print scale

steps.title: Create Scale Bar
steps.subtitle: Graphical scale representation
steps.why: Scale bar provides quick distance reference and remains accurate even if map is reproduced at different sizes.
instructions: Set current layer to SCALE_BAR
Draw scale bar using PLINE with appropriate divisions (e.g., 0, 50, 100, 200m)
Make primary divisions longer than secondary divisions for clarity
Add distance labels using TEXT (e.g., '0', '50m', '100m', '200m')
Add title 'SCALE' above the bar
Place scale bar in bottom corner, typically bottom right or bottom left

steps.title: Add Reference Grid
steps.subtitle: Coordinate grid for location reference
steps.why: Grid helps locate specific areas and is useful for municipal planning discussions.
instructions: Set current layer to GRID
Create grid lines at regular intervals (e.g., every 100m or 200m)
Add grid coordinates along edges (A,B,C or 1,2,3)
Use lighter lineweight so grid doesn't dominate the map
Ensure grid is visible but not distracting from main features

phases.phaseDescription: Add text labels, legend, and title information.

steps.title: Add Text Labels and Annotations
steps.subtitle: Identify roads, buildings, and landmarks
steps.why: Clear labels help reviewers quickly understand the map context and identify key features.
instructions: Set current layer to TEXT
Add road names, building names, and landmark labels
Use consistent text height (e.g., 3mm at print scale for main labels)
Position text to avoid overlapping with map features
Use QLEADER for detailed notes pointing to specific features
Add site name and project title prominently

steps.title: Create Legend
steps.subtitle: Explain symbols and patterns used
steps.why: Legend explains all symbols, colors, and patterns used in the map for clarity.
instructions: Create legend box in corner using RECTANG
List all symbols used with explanations (e.g., '■ Existing Building', '■ Proposed Site')
Include color/linetype explanations if multiple types used
Keep legend compact but complete
Place legend where it doesn't obscure important map areas

steps.title: Add Title Block Information
steps.subtitle: Project details and submission information
steps.why: Title block provides essential project identification and submission details required by municipalities.
instructions: Create title block in bottom or top margin
Include: Project Name, Location Map Title, Scale, Date
Add: Drawing Number, Prepared By, Checked By, Municipality Name
Include contact information and reference numbers
Use professional formatting and clear hierarchy

phases.phaseDescription: Review completeness and prepare for municipal submission.

steps.title: Verify Municipal Requirements
steps.subtitle: Check all mandatory elements
steps.why: Municipalities reject incomplete location maps. Ensure all requirements are met before submission.
instructions: Verify north arrow points to true north
Check scale bar is accurate and clearly labeled
Ensure site boundary is clearly highlighted
Confirm all text is readable at intended print scale
Check that surrounding context is adequate for project understanding

steps.title: Export for Submission
steps.subtitle: Create print-ready PDF with coordinate verification
steps.why: Municipalities require specific file formats and coordinate verification for official submissions.
instructions: Set up layout with appropriate paper size (A1 or A0 for large sites)
Include coordinate grid and GPS reference points in final output
Export to PDF with vector output for digital submissions
Create coordinate verification sheet showing key GPS points
Verify file naming: ProjectName_LocationMap_Date_Scale.pdf
Include metadata: coordinate system, datum, and survey source
submissionChecklist: GPS coordinates clearly marked at site corners
Scale bar and north arrow present and accurate
Coordinate system specified (WGS84/UTM Zone 45N for Nepal)
Survey license number and date included
All surrounding roads named and width dimensioned
File size under 10MB for digital submission
